name: James Galway
abstract: Sir James Galway  (born 8 December 1939) is an Irish virtuoso flute player from Belfast, nicknamed "The Man with the Golden Flute". After several years working as an orchestral musician, he established an international career as a solo flute player.

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I went to Eastman and studied with Bonita Boyd there... when I was auditioning to get into Eastman James Galway had just accepted the offer to teach there... it was actually good that I didn't study with him
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Most
about_quotes.evidence: one day James Galway came in with two gold flutes. He let me and my brother play his flutes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Don Menza and Sam Noto
about_quotes.evidence: James Galway is that way. He considers himself an interpretive artist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Menza and Sam Noto interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, January 28, 1997
